title: Bootstrap initialization seed (2026-02)
trigger: Workflow-memory coverage bootstrap for fresh repository/month baseline.
finding: Initialization-only seeded event added to satisfy strict coverage baseline. This does not represent an observed workflow run.
followUp: Continue normal workflow execution and append observed events; keep initialization entries only as baseline markers.
reflection: Initialization seed distinguishes bootstrap coverage from observed operational workflow activity.
feedback: If this command is needed repeatedly, investigate why observed events are not being persisted.

title: Added issue-evaluator automation lane and 5-minute loop
trigger: User request to replace manual issue approval with automated stewardship triage
finding: Introduced an issue-evaluator automation that triages all open GitHub issues and applies exactly one decision label (ready-for-dev, rejected, human-eval-needed) using value-vs-complexity and maintainability criteria
followUp: Install the new runtime wrapper in ~/.codex/automations/issue-evaluator/automation.toml and start pnpm issue-evaluator:loop; monitor first runs for label churn thresholds
reflection: Aligned approval behavior across automation docs and made ready-for-dev gate label-driven instead of human-only wording
feedback: Consider adding an issue-evaluator dry-run mode and changed-since timestamp guard to reduce repeated comments on stable issues

title: best-practice-researcher: baseline macro scan (repo-tooling-and-pipeline-config)
trigger: Human-requested automation run; first-memory baseline with no prior Periodic Scans entries
finding: Walk mode=weighted-random baseline; previous path=none; chosen path=macro/repo-tooling-and-pipeline-config; coverage snapshot last8=[]; strict coverage observed 0/5; opened issues #1 #2 #3 for CI gate contract, pnpm build-script policy, and workflow-memory bootstrap
evidence: Commands: gh auth status/repo view/issue list preflight passed; pnpm workflow-memory:retrieve returned 0 rows; pnpm workflow-memory:coverage:strict failed 0/5; references: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/use-cases-and-examples/building-and-testing/building-and-testing-nodejs, https://pnpm.io/cli/install, https://pnpm.io/cli/approve-builds, https://pnpm.io/settings#onlybuiltdependencies; issues: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/agent-engine-template/issues/1 https://github.com/brandonbryant12/agent-engine-template/issues/2 https://github.com/brandonbryant12/agent-engine-template/issues/3
followUp: Await human triage and ready-for-dev labeling on #1/#2/#3; next run should diversify path away from macro/repo-tooling unless unresolved high-priority signal remains
reflection: Followability delta +2: run produced concrete, approval-gated backlog with direct evidence and official references
feedback: Consider adding first-run memory bootstrap to reduce strict-coverage cold-start friction

title: ready-for-dev-executor run summary: issues #2 and #3
trigger: ready-for-dev label triage selected bundled issues #2 and #3
finding: Implemented and merged one bundled PR that codified pnpm build-script policy and added workflow-memory strict-coverage bootstrap.
followUp: Continue ready-for-dev triage; if strict-coverage bootstrap usage repeats, review missing observed event capture in active workflows.

title: Guarded PR closure keywords against markdown code formatting
trigger: Issue #45 reported PR #43 linkage text wrapped in code span
finding: ready-for-dev-executor policy allowed markdown-coded Fixes text to pass checks
followUp: Keep linkage gate validating non-code markdown and reject inline/fenced code keyword-only matches
reflection: Small policy hardening plus explicit gate language closed the recurrence path quickly
feedback: Consider a future script-level parser test to enforce this automatically

title: Shipped prompt registry for chat general
finding: Implemented in-repo prompt artifacts + resolver precedence, lifecycle statuses, typed failures, compatibility fallback, and chat migration.
followUp: Track fallback telemetry and remove legacy-inline-fallback after 14 days of zero fallback events.
reflection: Bundled resolver + migration + tests in one vertical slice with guardrail gates.
feedback: Keep resolver schema typing strict while avoiding explicit any; prefer narrowed union assertions in tests.

title: Prompt registry rollout completed for chat
finding: Used existing chat/prompts architecture on main, added resolver observability fields, removed inline prompt constant from use-case, and documented lifecycle + compatibility sunset.
followUp: Track fallback telemetry and remove legacy-inline fallback after 30 days with zero fallback usage.
reflection: Rebasing onto latest main before merge prevented duplicate prompt subsystems and kept architecture coherent.
feedback: For long-running automation runs, re-check origin/main before merge to detect overlapping landings sooner.

title: best-practice-researcher: meso scan (observability-otel-runtime-lifecycle)
trigger: Scheduled automation run after baseline macro repo-tooling pass; diversify weighted random walk with external research
finding: Walk mode=weighted-random; previous path=macro/repo-tooling-and-pipeline-config; chosen path=meso/observability-otel-runtime-lifecycle; identified three high-signal lifecycle gaps and opened issues #56 #57 #58
evidence: Repo evidence: apps/server/src/server.ts:79-120 and apps/worker/src/worker.ts:99-132 force process.exit(0) even on shutdown errors; no lifecycle invariants found by rg over __tests__. Duplicate check via gh issue/pr search across open+closed; no existing shutdown/telemetry lifecycle issue found. Research: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/languages/js/getting-started/nodejs/ ; https://nodejs.org/api/process.html#processexitcode ; https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-js/blob/main/doc/tracing.md . Issues opened: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/agent-engine-template/issues/56 https://github.com/brandonbryant12/agent-engine-template/issues/57 https://github.com/brandonbryant12/agent-engine-template/issues/58
followUp: Await issue-evaluator/human triage and ready-for-dev labeling on #56-#58; next run should continue diversity away from same meso domain unless high-priority follow-up emerges
reflection: Followability delta +2: recommendations tied to concrete line-level evidence plus official OTel/Node references and explicit acceptance criteria
feedback: Add invariant coverage for entrypoint lifecycle hooks earlier to reduce repeated manual validation in periodic scans
